page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-2 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the c2d server family. It is configured with 2 vCPUs mapped to 1 physical core of an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor using the x86_64 architecture. The instance features dedicated CPU allocation with 2 threads per core and includes 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPU hardware, and it offers zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct performance profile characterized by strong single-core capabilities but constrained multi-core and memory throughput. In stress-ng testing, single-core CPU performance ranks in the top 25 percent, whereas multi-core performance falls into the bottom 25 percent, reflecting the physical limitation of its single-core, dual-thread design. Memory performance is a primary bottleneck; the instance registers poor memory bandwidth on small cached blocks and weak bandwidth on larger uncached blocks. Similarly, Passmark memory and database operation scores fall into the bottom 10 percent, and Redis SET operations are limited to 2.32 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c2d-highcpu-2 offers a low-cost entry point into dedicated AMD EPYC compute resources, but it presents significant resource-density tradeoffs. The low memory-to-vCPU ratio and restricted memory bandwidth make it poorly suited for memory-intensive applications, database hosting, or complex parallel compilation. However, its strong single-core performance and average single-threaded compression speeds make it appropriate for lightweight, single-threaded workloads, basic web serving, and small-scale background tasks that require dedicated CPU execution without high memory overhead.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-2 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs derived from 1 physical core, 4.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core CPU execution, placing in the top 25 percent in stress-ng tests, but multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are weak, scoring in the bottom 25 percent and 10 percent respectively. This resource profile creates a clear tradeoff: while the instance provides cost-effective access to dedicated AMD EPYC execution, its low memory capacity and restricted bandwidth limit its utility for database or memory-heavy tasks. It is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded applications, basic web serving, and small-scale background processing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-2 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance with 2 vCPUs, 4 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7B13 CPU, offering strong single-core performance but limited memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-2 is a 2-vCPU, 4 GB RAM compute-optimized instance featuring an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor and dedicated CPU cores.
tagline: A dedicated dual-vCPU compute instance featuring strong single-core execution and a compact memory footprint for lightweight, single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with x86_64 architecture
Configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 4.0 GB of system memory
Strong single-core CPU performance ranking in the top 25 percent
Constrained memory bandwidth and multi-core scaling tradeoffs
Best suited for lightweight single-threaded tasks and basic web serving
